    <h1 {...{
      "id": "using-document-sandbox-constants"
    }}>{`Using Document Sandbox Constants`}</h1>
    <p>{`Document Sandbox constants provide type-safe ways to interact with the Document APIs for content creation like fills, strokes, text styling, and node manipulation. This guide covers the most common patterns for document sandbox development.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "why-use-constants"
    }}>{`Why Use Constants?`}</h2>
    <p>{`Constants equal their variable name as a string (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`FillType.color`}</inlineCode>{` equals `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"color"`}</inlineCode>{`), but using constants provides type safety, IDE autocomplete, and future-proofing against API changes. They ensure consistency when working with document elements, styling, and content creation.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert slots="header, text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Add-on UI vs. Document Sandbox constants`}</p>
    <p>{`Separate sets of constants are available in both the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "ui-sdk-constants.md"
      }}>{`Add-on UI`}</a>{` and the Document Sandbox environments. For the complete technical specification on the constants covered in this guide, see the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/document-apis/enumerations/ArrowHeadType.md"
      }}>{`Document APIs Constants Reference`}</a></p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "quick-start"
    }}>{`Quick Start`}</h2>
    <p>{`Document constants are available through the constants export in the document sandbox environment:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// Access constants through the constants object
const fillType = constants.FillType.color;
const blendMode = constants.BlendMode.normal;
const textAlignment = constants.TextAlignment.center;
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert slots="header, text1" variant="warning" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Important`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Document constants are only available in the document sandbox environment (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{`). They cannot be accessed from the iframe UI environment.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "most-common-use-cases"
    }}>{`Most Common Use Cases`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "fill-and-stroke-properties"
    }}>{`Fill and Stroke Properties`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// Creating solid color fills
const rectangle = editor.createRectangle();
rectangle.fill = {
  type: constants.FillType.color,
  color: { red: 1, green: 0, blue: 0, alpha: 1 }
};

// Adding strokes
rectangle.stroke = {
  type: constants.StrokeType.color,
  color: { red: 0, green: 0, blue: 1, alpha: 1 },
  width: 2,
  position: constants.StrokePosition.inside
};
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Available Fill Types:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`FillType.color`}</inlineCode>{` - Solid color fills (only available type)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Available Stroke Types:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`StrokeType.color`}</inlineCode>{` - Solid color stroke (only available type)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Available Stroke Positions:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`StrokePosition.center`}</inlineCode>{` - Stroke centered on edge`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`StrokePosition.inside`}</inlineCode>{` - Stroke inside the shape`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`StrokePosition.outside`}</inlineCode>{` - Stroke outside the shape`}</li>
    </ul>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "scene-node-types"

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "working-with-colors"
    }}>{`Working with Colors`}</h2>
    <p>{`When working with fill and stroke properties, you'll need to provide Color objects. Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{` utility from `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`express-document-sdk`}</inlineCode>{` to create colors:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { editor, constants, colorUtils } from "express-document-sdk";

// Create colors and use with constants
const rectangle = editor.createRectangle();
rectangle.fill = {
  type: constants.FillType.color,           // Constant for type safety
  color: colorUtils.fromHex("#FF0000")      // Color from hex string
};

rectangle.stroke = {
  type: constants.StrokeType.color,
  color: colorUtils.fromRGB(0, 0, 1),       // Color from RGB values
  width: 2,
  position: constants.StrokePosition.inside
};
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert slots="text" variant="info" mdxType="InlineAlert" />

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "common-patterns"
    }}>{`Common Patterns`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "creating-styled-shapes"
    }}>{`Creating Styled Shapes`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

function createStyledRectangle(color, strokeColor) {
  const rect = editor.createRectangle();

  // Set fill
  rect.fill = {
    type: constants.FillType.color,
    color: color
  };

  // Set stroke
  rect.stroke = {
    type: constants.StrokeType.color,
    color: strokeColor,
    width: 2,
    position: constants.StrokePosition.inside
  };

  // Set blend mode
  rect.blendMode = constants.BlendMode.normal;

  return rect;
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "text-formatting"
    }}>{`Text Formatting`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

function createFormattedText(content, alignment = constants.TextAlignment.left) {
  const textNode = editor.createText();
  textNode.text = content;
  textNode.textAlignment = alignment;

  // Apply character styling
  const characterStyles = {
    fontSize: 24,
    fontFamily: "Arial",
    textScriptStyle: constants.TextScriptStyle.none
  };

  textNode.setRangeCharacterStyles(0, content.length, characterStyles);

  return textNode;
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "node-type-checking"
    }}>{`Node Type Checking`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { editor, constants } from "express-document-sdk";

function processSelectedNodes() {
  const selection = editor.context.selection;

  selection.forEach(node => {
    // Type-safe node processing
    if (node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.rectangle || node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.ellipse) {
      // Handle shapes
      if (node.fill?.type === constants.FillType.color) {
        console.log("Shape has color fill");
      }
    } else if (node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.imageRectangle) {
      // Handle images
      console.log("Processing image node");
    }
  });
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "common-pitfalls"

    <h3 {...{

    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// ❌ Wrong - Document constants not available in UI

`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "missing-type-checks"
    }}>{`Missing Type Checks`}</h3>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`import { constants } from "express-document-sdk";

// ❌ Risky - assuming node type
function changeColor(node, color) {
  node.fill = { type: constants.FillType.color, color }; // May fail on non-fillable nodes
}

// ✅ Safe - check node type first
function changeColor(node, color) {
  if (node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.rectangle || node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.ellipse) {
    node.fill = { type: constants.FillType.color, color };
  }
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-whats-the-difference-between-ui-sdk-constants-and-document-sandbox-constants"
    }}>{`Q: What's the difference between UI SDK constants and Document Sandbox constants?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` UI SDK constants are for iframe operations (dialogs, exports, events). Document constants are for content creation (fills, strokes, text alignment, node types).`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-use-filltypegradient-or-other-fill-types"

    <h4 {...{

    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Check the node type first: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`if (node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.rectangle || node.type === constants.SceneNodeType.ellipse)`}</inlineCode>{` before applying fill/stroke properties.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
